course_title: British Literature II: Revolution and Romance
description:
Offered: every semester
This course examines British literature from the Restoration, Eighteenth-Century, and Romantic eras (1660-1834). The course considers how ‘classical’ and ‘romantic’ authors think about literary authority, influence, and imagination. It also explores cultural and historical contexts as they shape British literature and thought during these eras, with a focus on issues of race, class, and gender.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Note: Students who have declared an English Studies major may take concurrently with ENGL 1200 with department approval.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, LI
